audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for imitation, head/source/order, praying/prophesying, mutuality in Lord, Lord's supper abuse, discerning body.

verses.restored: Become imitators of me, just as I also am of Messiah.
I praise you because you remember me in all things and hold fast the traditions just as I handed them over to you.
But I want you to know that the head/source of every man is Messiah, and head/source of woman is man, and head/source of Messiah is the Divine.
Every man praying or prophesying having something down over the head dishonors his head/source.
But every woman praying or prophesying with uncovered head dishonors her head/source; for it is one and the same as having been shaved.
For if a woman is not covered, let her also be shorn; but if it is shameful for a woman to be shorn or shaved, let her be covered.
For a man indeed ought not cover the head, being image and glory of the Divine; but woman is glory of man.
For man is not from woman, but woman from man;
for also man was not created because of the woman, but woman because of the man.
Because of this the woman ought to have authority upon her head, because of the messengers.
Nevertheless, neither woman apart from man nor man apart from woman in the Lord.
For just as the woman is from the man, so also the man is through the woman; but all things are from the Divine.
Judge among yourselves: is it fitting for a woman to pray to the Divine uncovered?
Does not nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is dishonor to him,
but if a woman has long hair, it is glory to her? For the hair has been given to her instead of a covering.
But if anyone seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor do the assemblies of the Divine.
But in commanding this, I do not praise, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.
For first, when you come together in assembly, I hear there are splits among you, and in part I trust it.
For it is necessary that factions also be among you, so that the approved ones may become manifest among you.
When you therefore come together in the same place, it is not to eat the Lord's supper.
For each one takes one's own supper beforehand in the eating, and one is hungry while another is drunk.
Do you not have houses for eating and drinking? Or do you despise the assembly of the Divine and shame those having nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I do not praise.
For I received from the Lord what I also handed over to you, that the Lord Jesus, in the night he was handed over, took bread,
and giving thanks, broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this into my remembrance.
Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this, as often as you drink, into my remembrance.
For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the death of the Lord until he comes.
Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord unworthily will be liable concerning the body and blood of the Lord.
Let a human examine oneself, and thus let one eat from the bread and drink from the cup.
For the one eating and drinking eats and drinks judgment to oneself if not discerning the body.
Because of this many among you are weak and sick, and a considerable number sleep.
But if we discerned ourselves, we would not be judged.
But being judged by the Lord, we are disciplined, so that we may not be condemned with the world.
So then, my siblings, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
If anyone is hungry, let that one eat at home, so that you may not come together into judgment. The remaining things I will set in order when I come.

translation_notes: Kephale is rendered head/source to preserve the debated range of headship/source/order language.
Women are explicitly praying and prophesying in the assembly; the issue is ordered honor, not silencing.
Discerning the body includes the communal body, especially the poor shamed at the meal.
